description: Sync your Amazon order history into a local SQLite store, then ask cross-cutting questions offline: which deliveries are slipping, what did I spend last quarter, when did I order that thing, where is my stuff right now.
auth_description:
Amazon does not publish a buyer-side API for order history. This CLI imports
cookies from your logged-in Chrome / Firefox / Safari / Brave browser session,
then makes authenticated HTTPS GETs to the same HTML pages you see on
amazon.com. Log in to amazon.com in your browser, then run:

  amazon-orders-pp-cli auth login --chrome

Cookies live under the .amazon.com domain. The CLI persists them locally and
refreshes the session-id cookie automatically when amazon.com rotates it.

novel_features.name: Where Is My Stuff
novel_features.description: All in-flight Amazon shipments with their current status and ETA in one view.

novel_features.name: Delivery Slips Detector
novel_features.description: Orders whose actual delivery date slipped more than N days from the original estimate.

novel_features.name: Spend Rollup
novel_features.description: Spending broken down by month, year, category, seller, or payment method.

novel_features.name: Top Items
novel_features.description: Most-ordered items by frequency or by total spend, ASIN-grouped across all history.

novel_features.name: Subscribe & Save Detector
novel_features.description: Recurring purchases inferred from order history (same ASIN ordered on a regular cadence).

novel_features.name: Find That Thing
novel_features.description: FTS5 search across orders, items, sellers, and tracking notes.

novel_features.name: Arriving Soon
novel_features.description: Shipments arriving in the next N days, sorted by ETA.

novel_features.name: Late Shipments
novel_features.description: Active shipments past their original estimated delivery date.
